name: Boston Banana Cream Pie
recipeInstructions:
Cake: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F. Grease bottom of 9-inch pie pan. Line bottom with waxed paper and grease again. In a large bowl, blend together flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, milk, butter, vanilla, and egg until moistened. Beat on medium speed for 2 minutes. Pour into pie pan. Bake for 20-30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Cool for 5 minutes. Invert onto a wire rack. Remove waxed paper. Cool completely. Split horizontally into two thin layers.
Filling: In a medium saucepan, mix together sugar, flour, and salt. Gradually stir in milk. Cook over low heat until boiling for 1 minute. Stir constantly. Blend in the hot mixture with egg and return it to the saucepan. Cook until bubbly, stirring constantly. Remove from heat. Stir in butter and vanilla. Cool. Spread half of the filling on the cut side of the larger cake layer. Arrange banana slices over the filling. Spread remaining filling on top. Place the cake layer with the cut side down.
Glaze: In a small saucepan over low heat, melt chocolate and 2 tablespoons butter. Remove from heat. Stir in powdered sugar, salt, almond extract, and enough milk to achieve a spreading consistency. Mix until smooth. Spread over the top of the cake, allowing glaze to drip down the sides. Refrigerate before serving. Store in refrigerator.
